sections.paragraph:
Τράλλεις, εων, αἱ (X., An. 1, 4, 8 al.; ins. It occurs mostly in the pl. form [X.; Diod S 14, 36, 3; Jos., Ant. 14, 245; OGI 441, 162], though the sing. Τράλλις, ιος [epigram in Agathias Hist. p. 102, 15; Stephan. Byz. s.v.; SibOr 3, 459; 5, 289] is not impossible) Tralles, a city in Caria (southwest Asia Minor), north of the Maeander River, ITr ins.—KHumann/WDorpfeld, MAI 18, 1893, 395ff; JWeiss, RE X 547; VSchultze, Altchristliche Städte und Landschaften II/2, 1926; GBean, Turkey beyond the Maeander ’71, 208–11; Pauly-W. VI 2093–128; Kl. Pauly V 922; resident Jews, Schürer III 24, 167; PECS 931.

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5
  2. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

  1. pl. = plural

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

OGI OGI = Orientis Graeci Inscriptiones Selectae—List 3

sing. sing. = singular

Agathias Agathias , VI a.d.—List 5

Hist.

Hist. (after a personal name)=historicus, historian

hist. = historian, historical

Byz. Byz. = Byzantine, esp. in ref. to papyri

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

SibOr SibOr = Oracula Sibyllina, II–III a.d.—List 5

ITr ITr = Ignatius to the Trallians—List 1

MAI MAI = Mitteilungen des Deutschen Archäologischen Instituts—Lists 3, 6

RE RE = Realencyclopädie für protestantische Theologie und Kirche—List 6

Pauly-W Pauly-W = AP./GW., Real-Encyclopädie der klassischen Altertumswissenschaft—List 6

Kl. Pauly Kl. Pauly = Der Kleine Pauly: Lexikon der Antike—List 6

PECS PECS = Princeton Encyclopedia of Classical Sites—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 1013
